title: Picrophloeus rugulosus
taxonomy: Picrophloeus rugulosus is a member of the plant family Euphorbiaceae and is classified under the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales, a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is further classified into the genus Picrophloeus, a monotypic genus that consists of a single species. Picrophloeus rugulosus is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and is often found growing in rocky and sandy areas. The classification of Picrophloeus rugulosus is an important aspect of its identification and understanding of its evolutionary history.

intro: Picrophloeus rugulosus, also known as Cape sumac, is a perennial shrub native to southern Africa, characterized by its bright red berries and distinctive, branching stems. Traditionally, Cape sumac has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cape sumac include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cape sumac is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
